content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ whose elements are zeros and ones.

A sequence that consists of LLL integers is called a permutation if and only if each integer from 111 to LLL appears exactly once in it.

You need to find any permutation PPP of integers from 111 to NNN for which it holds that for every valid index iii, Ai=1A_i=1Ai​=1 if the prefix of PPP of length iii is a permutation of numbers from 111 to iii and Ai=0A_i=0Ai​=0 otherwise, or determine that such a permutation does not exist.


Input Format


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.




The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.





Output Format


For each test case, print a single line.




In case a suitable permutation PPP does not exist, print a single integer −1-1−1.




Otherwise, print NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 4·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1,A2,…,AN (each 0 or 1). It is guaranteed that AN=1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line. If no permutation satisfying the conditions exists, print -1. Otherwise print N space‑separated integers P1,P2,…,PN forming a valid permutation.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we construct the permutation by outputting each segment ending at a position i with Ai=1 in decreasing order. This yields the shown permutations, and every prefix i satisfies Ai=1 iff the prefix is a permutation of 1..i.

solution.approach: We scan the array from left to right. Let last be the index of the previous position where Ai=1 (initially 0). Whenever we encounter Ai=1 at position i, the segment (last+1 … i) must contain exactly the numbers last+1 … i, otherwise the prefix i would not be a permutation of 1..i. To guarantee that for every index k in this segment with k<i the prefix is NOT a permutation (because Ak=0), we output the numbers of the segment in decreasing order: i, i‑1, …, last+1. This makes the first element of any unfinished prefix larger than its length, breaking the permutation property. After processing the segment we set last=i and continue. If AN≠1 we output -1 (the statement guarantees AN=1, but we keep the check). The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case and uses O(N) additional memory for the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
